GhostVLAD is an extension of the NetVLAD approach, which adds Ghost clusters along with the NetVLAD clusters. The Ghost clusters are added to map any noisy or irrelevant content into ghost clusters and are not included during the feature aggregation stage.

Based on the text, the GhostVLAD approach is an extension of the NetVLAD pooling strategy that adds "Ghost clusters" alongside normal NetVLAD clusters. These Ghost clusters are designed to absorb noisy or irrelevant content during the computation of the feature matrix but are excluded during the final feature aggregation stage, thereby reducing the weight of unwanted features and improving language discrimination.
answers: extension of the NetVLAD, adds Ghost clusters along with the NetVLAD clusters
An extension of NetVLAD which replaces hard assignment-based clustering with soft assignment-based clustering with the additon o fusing Ghost clusters to deal with noisy content.
